dialogues: This bar shows 45, between the lines. How did you figure that out during the lesson?
Right. Half of 10 is 5, so halfway between any two lines is 5 more than the lower line.
Knowing that pattern means you can read any bar graph with scale of 10, even values between the lines. That's a powerful strategy.
You learned to read values at the lines and between the lines. You used 'half of 10 is 5' to figure out tricky values.
That pattern-finding will help you when we look at all the scales together next time. You'll be able to pick the best one for any data.
